meta.title: G7 Presidency History: Annual Leaders’ Meetings, 1975–2026
meta.purpose: Policy-history dataset prepared to support advance work for the 2027 United States G7 presidency.
meta.scope: One record for each annual summit cycle from the first G6 meeting in 1975 through the completed 2026 G7 summit at Evian. Extraordinary inter-summit calls and ministerial meetings are excluded except where a note is essential to understanding the annual summit record.
meta.source_note: Recent links point to host-government records where available. Many older links point to the University of Toronto G7 Research Group's archive of official summit texts; those records are marked archived_official_text.
meta.uncertainty_note: Null venue values are intentional where a precise meeting site was not established confidently from the reviewed sources. Themes are concise editorial summaries of the linked outcome documents, not quotations.

summits.name: Rambouillet
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on coordinating recovery from recession, high unemployment, and inflation; improving monetary cooperation and responding to disorderly exchange-rate movements; and reducing vulnerability to energy shocks while conserving oil.
summits.context: The first summit and the only annual summit held as the G6: France, West Germany, Italy, Japan, the United Kingdom, and the United States.
outcomes: Coordinate recovery from recession, high unemployment, and inflation
Improve monetary cooperation and respond to disorderly exchange-rate movements
Reduce vulnerability to energy shocks while conserving oil
Keep markets open and resist protectionism
summits.why_it_matters: The first summit and the only annual summit held as the G6: France, West Germany, Italy, Japan, the United Kingdom, and the United States.

documents.title: Declaration of Rambouillet
documents.publisher: G7 Research Group, University of Toronto

data_gaps: The original host-government page is not registered; the linked official summit text is accessed through the University of Toronto documentary archive.

summits.name: San Juan
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on sustaining economic expansion while reducing unemployment and inflation; avoiding both excessive stimulus and premature restraint; and completing the Tokyo Round of multilateral trade negotiations.
summits.context: Canada joined, converting the G6 into the G7. The summit is conventionally called San Juan, although the declaration identifies Dorado Beach as the meeting place.
outcomes: Sustain economic expansion while reducing unemployment and inflation
Avoid both excessive stimulus and premature restraint
Complete the Tokyo Round of multilateral trade negotiations
Coordinate energy and international monetary policy
Strengthen cooperation with developing countries and international institutions
summits.why_it_matters: Canada joined, converting the G6 into the G7. The summit is conventionally called San Juan, although the declaration identifies Dorado Beach as the meeting place.

summits.name: London I
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on setting country-specific growth objectives to create jobs without reigniting inflation; advancing the Tokyo Round and resisting protectionism; and conserve energy and expanding alternative and nuclear supplies.
summits.context: The European Community began participating through its institutional representatives, an arrangement that evolved into today's European Union participation.
outcomes: Set country-specific growth objectives to create jobs without reigniting inflation
Advance the Tokyo Round and resist protectionism
Conserve energy and expand alternative and nuclear supplies
Coordinate with the IMF, World Bank, GATT, and OECD
summits.why_it_matters: The European Community began participating through its institutional representatives, an arrangement that evolved into today's European Union participation.

summits.name: Bonn I
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on adopting a coordinated package of country-specific measures for growth, jobs, and inflation control; setting oil-import restraint goals for the United States and European Community; and finishing the Tokyo Round by the end of 1978.
summits.context: This established g7 meeting paired adopting a coordinated package of country-specific measures for growth, jobs, and inflation control with setting oil-import restraint goals for the United States and European Community.
outcomes: Adopt a coordinated package of country-specific measures for growth, jobs, and inflation control
Set oil-import restraint goals for the United States and European Community
Finish the Tokyo Round by the end of 1978
Condemn international air hijacking and strengthen collective responses
summits.why_it_matters: Shows how the established G7 coordinated macroeconomic policy while progressively widening its work to energy, development, security, and social issues.

summits.name: Tokyo I
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on setting quantified ceilings on oil imports and accelerating conservation; develop coal, nuclear power, and other alternatives to imported oil; and coordinating growth, inflation, and balance-of-payments policies.
summits.context: This established g7 meeting paired setting quantified ceilings on oil imports and accelerating conservation with develop coal, nuclear power, and other alternatives to imported oil.
outcomes: Set quantified ceilings on oil imports and accelerate conservation
Develop coal, nuclear power, and other alternatives to imported oil
Coordinate growth, inflation, and balance-of-payments policies
Respond collectively to the Indochinese refugee crisis
summits.why_it_matters: Shows how the established G7 coordinated macroeconomic policy while progressively widening its work to energy, development, security, and social issues.

summits.name: Venice I
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on making inflation control the leading economic priority while supporting investment and innovation; reducing oil consumption and dependence through national energy programs; and implementing the Tokyo Round and resisting new trade restrictions.
summits.context: Political-security statements became a more visible part of the summit record alongside economic coordination.
outcomes: Make inflation control the leading economic priority while supporting investment and innovation
Reduce oil consumption and dependence through national energy programs
Implement the Tokyo Round and resist new trade restrictions
Address Afghanistan, diplomatic hostages, refugees, and hijacking in separate political statements
summits.why_it_matters: Political-security statements became a more visible part of the summit record alongside economic coordination.

summits.name: Ottawa-Montebello
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on restoring sustainable growth while reducing inflation and unemployment; limiting destabilizing exchange-rate fluctuations; and strengthening energy security and conservation.
summits.context: Often listed as the Ottawa Summit; the principal retreat was at Montebello, Quebec. Leaders remained divided over aspects of East-West trade.
outcomes: Restore sustainable growth while reducing inflation and unemployment
Limit destabilizing exchange-rate fluctuations
Strengthen energy security and conservation
Advance North-South cooperation and development
Recognize environmental protection as an economic policy concern
summits.why_it_matters: Often listed as the Ottawa Summit; the principal retreat was at Montebello, Quebec. Leaders remained divided over aspects of East-West trade.

summits.name: Versailles
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on seeking recovery and lowering unemployment without sacrificing price stability; strengthening monetary cooperation and the IMF's role; and maintaining an open trading system amid recession.
summits.context: This established g7 meeting paired seeking recovery and lowering unemployment without sacrificing price stability with strengthening monetary cooperation and the IMF's role.
outcomes: Seek recovery and lower unemployment without sacrificing price stability
Strengthen monetary cooperation and the IMF's role
Maintain an open trading system amid recession
Coordinate cautious approaches to East-West economic relations
Promote technology, energy diversification, and development
summits.why_it_matters: Shows how the established G7 coordinated macroeconomic policy while progressively widening its work to energy, development, security, and social issues.

summits.name: Tokyo II
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on promoting durable growth through structural reform and disciplined public spending; creating regular G7 finance-minister coordination and stronger multilateral surveillance; and advancing the new multilateral trade round.
summits.context: The summit institutionalized more regular G7 finance-minister surveillance between leaders' meetings.
outcomes: Promote durable growth through structural reform and disciplined public spending
Create regular G7 finance-minister coordination and stronger multilateral surveillance
Advance the new multilateral trade round
Respond to the Chernobyl accident with nuclear-safety cooperation
Strengthen action against international terrorism
summits.why_it_matters: The summit institutionalized more regular G7 finance-minister surveillance between leaders' meetings.

summits.name: Toronto
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on sustaining non-inflationary growth through coordinated policies; advancing the Uruguay Round and resisting protectionism; and offering more generous debt relief to the poorest countries through the Toronto terms.
summits.context: The 'Toronto terms' became an important milestone in official bilateral debt relief for low-income countries.
outcomes: Sustain non-inflationary growth through coordinated policies
Advance the Uruguay Round and resist protectionism
Offer more generous debt relief to the poorest countries through the Toronto terms
Increase environmental cooperation
Coordinate on regional conflicts, human rights, and arms control
summits.why_it_matters: The 'Toronto terms' became an important milestone in official bilateral debt relief for low-income countries.

summits.name: Paris
summits.summit_title: Summit of the Arch / Paris Summit
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on maintaining balanced growth while containing inflation and unemployment; completing the Uruguay Round and integrating developing economies into world trade; and expanding debt relief and poverty-oriented development.
summits.context: Leaders from fifteen developing countries met with the G7 on the eve of the summit.
outcomes: Maintain balanced growth while containing inflation and unemployment
Complete the Uruguay Round and integrate developing economies into world trade
Expand debt relief and poverty-oriented development
Make environmental protection, ozone depletion, and climate a summit priority
Support democratic and economic reform in Poland and Hungary
Address the Tiananmen crackdown through a separate China declaration
summits.why_it_matters: Leaders from fifteen developing countries met with the G7 on the eve of the summit.

summits.name: London III
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on supporting sustained recovery, price stability, and lowering interest rates; completing the Uruguay Round through direct leaders' engagement if necessary; and building a post-Cold War international partnership and strengthening the United Nations.
summits.context: Gorbachev joined a post-summit dialogue, beginning a progressively deeper pattern of Russian participation.
outcomes: Support sustained recovery, price stability, and lower interest rates
Complete the Uruguay Round through direct leaders' engagement if necessary
Build a post-Cold War international partnership and strengthen the United Nations
Engage Mikhail Gorbachev on Soviet political and economic reform
Control conventional arms transfers and nuclear, biological, and chemical proliferation
summits.why_it_matters: Gorbachev joined a post-summit dialogue, beginning a progressively deeper pattern of Russian participation.

summits.name: Munich
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on restoring growth and employment while reducing deficits and interest rates; promoting exchange-rate stability and stronger private investment; and completing the Uruguay Round.
summits.context: This post-cold war transition meeting paired restoring growth and employment while reducing deficits and interest rates with promoting exchange-rate stability and stronger private investment.
outcomes: Restore growth and employment while reducing deficits and interest rates
Promote exchange-rate stability and stronger private investment
Complete the Uruguay Round
Forge a new partnership with Russia and the other post-communist states
Respond to war in the former Yugoslavia
Integrate environmental goals into economic policy
summits.why_it_matters: Tracks the G7 adaptation to the end of the Cold War, post-communist transitions, and Russia's gradual incorporation into summit diplomacy.

summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on creating jobs and reviving growth through structural reform; completing the Uruguay Round after a major market-access agreement; and provide substantial support for Russian reform.
summits.context: Some host materials include preparatory events beginning July 6; the standard leaders' summit dates are July 7-9.

Complete the Uruguay Round after a major market-access agreement
Provide substantial support for Russian reform
Strengthen United Nations peacekeeping and peacebuilding
Coordinate against terrorism and weapons proliferation

summits.name: Naples
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on using structural reform, education, training, and innovation to improve jobs and growth; implementing the Uruguay Round and prepare the World Trade Organization; and develop the emerging global information society.
summits.context: Russia participated fully in political discussions, often described as the P8 or G7 plus one, while the economic core remained G7.
outcomes: Use structural reform, education, training, and innovation to improve jobs and growth
Implement the Uruguay Round and prepare the World Trade Organization
Develop the emerging global information society
Support reform and nuclear safety in Russia and other transition states
Address Bosnia, Croatia, and other regional conflicts
summits.why_it_matters: Russia participated fully in political discussions, often described as the P8 or G7 plus one, while the economic core remained G7.

summits.name: Lyon
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on protecting growth and international monetary stability; creating a new development partnership with developing countries and multilateral institutions; and continuing reform of the IMF, World Bank, United Nations, and other international bodies.
summits.context: A separate G7-with-Russia Nuclear Safety and Security Summit met in Moscow on April 19-20, 1996; this record covers the regular annual summit at Lyon.
outcomes: Protect growth and international monetary stability
Create a new development partnership with developing countries and multilateral institutions
Continue reform of the IMF, World Bank, United Nations, and other international bodies
Advance open trade and World Trade Organization implementation
Increase cooperation against terrorism
Support peace implementation in Bosnia and Herzegovina
summits.why_it_matters: A separate G7-with-Russia Nuclear Safety and Security Summit met in Moscow on April 19-20, 1996; this record covers the regular annual summit at Lyon.

summits.name: Denver
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on sustaining growth and employment while integrating Russia into summit governance; responding to population aging and supporting small and medium-sized enterprises; and promoting sustainable development and stronger environmental institutions.
summits.context: Russia joined the leaders' political deliberations as the 'Summit of the Eight,' but some economic and financial work continued in a G7 format.

Respond to population aging and support small and medium-sized enterprises
Promote sustainable development and stronger environmental institutions
Establish an Africa-focused development strategy
Advance United Nations reform, democracy, human rights, and arms control
Expand cooperation on the information society and transnational health threats

summits.name: Birmingham
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on responding to the Asian financial crisis and design a stronger international financial architecture; advancing debt relief for the poorest countries; and improving employability and social inclusion.
summits.context: Birmingham is generally treated as the first regular annual summit under the G8 name. A more informal leaders-only retreat became central to the format.
outcomes: Respond to the Asian financial crisis and design a stronger international financial architecture
Advance debt relief for the poorest countries
Improve employability and social inclusion
Combat drugs, organized crime, and other transnational threats
Condemn Indian and Pakistani nuclear tests and address crises including Kosovo and Indonesia
summits.why_it_matters: Birmingham is generally treated as the first regular annual summit under the G8 name. A more informal leaders-only retreat became central to the format.

summits.name: Cologne
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on stabilize the world economy after the Asian and Russian financial crises; launching faster, deeper, and broader debt relief through the Cologne Debt Initiative; and promoting jobs, lifelong learning, and social safeguards in a globalizing economy.
summits.context: The enhanced HIPC framework agreed at Cologne linked debt relief more explicitly to poverty reduction, health, education, and social spending.
outcomes: Stabilize the world economy after the Asian and Russian financial crises
Launch faster, deeper, and broader debt relief through the Cologne Debt Initiative
Promote jobs, lifelong learning, and social safeguards in a globalizing economy
Strengthen the international financial architecture
Support peace implementation, refugee return, and reconstruction in Kosovo and Southeast Europe
summits.why_it_matters: The enhanced HIPC framework agreed at Cologne linked debt relief more explicitly to poverty reduction, health, education, and social spending.

Adopt the Okinawa Charter on the Global Information Society and narrow the digital divide
Implement enhanced HIPC debt relief and poverty-reduction strategies

summits.name: Genoa
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on making globalization more inclusive while sustaining growth and financial stability; launching the Genoa Plan for Africa and advancing debt relief; and supporting creation of the Global Fund to fight AIDS, tuberculosis, and malaria.
summits.context: Large anti-globalization protests and the death of a protester shaped public memory of the summit and prompted later hosts to use more isolated venues.

Launch the Genoa Plan for Africa and advance debt relief
Support creation of the Global Fund to fight AIDS, tuberculosis, and malaria
Advance a new World Trade Organization round and food security
Expand digital opportunity and environmental cooperation
summits.why_it_matters: Large anti-globalization protests and the death of a protester shaped public memory of the summit and prompted later hosts to use more isolated venues.

summits.name: Kananaskis
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on adopting the G8 Africa Action Plan in response to NEPAD; launching the Global Partnership Against the Spread of Weapons and Materials of Mass Destruction; and strengthening counterterrorism and transport security after the September 11 attacks.
summits.context: Leaders announced that Russia would host in 2006, completing its progression to a full hosting role in the G8.
outcomes: Adopt the G8 Africa Action Plan in response to NEPAD
Launch the Global Partnership Against the Spread of Weapons and Materials of Mass Destruction
Strengthen counterterrorism and transport security after the September 11 attacks
Deliver enhanced HIPC debt relief and support development
Protect recovery and advance trade liberalization
summits.why_it_matters: Leaders announced that Russia would host in 2006, completing its progression to a full hosting role in the G8.

summits.name: Évian
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on restoring confidence and global growth after economic slowdown and the Iraq rupture; advancing the Africa Action Plan, health, water, and famine prevention; and strengthening WMD nonproliferation and securing radioactive sources.
summits.context: The summit worked to restore practical cooperation after deep divisions over the U.S.-led invasion of Iraq.
outcomes: Restore confidence and global growth after economic slowdown and the Iraq rupture
Advance the Africa Action Plan, health, water, and famine prevention
Strengthen WMD nonproliferation and secure radioactive sources
Build international capacity against terrorism and improve transport security
Fight corruption and increase transparency
Promote science, technology, and environmental sustainability
summits.why_it_matters: The summit worked to restore practical cooperation after deep divisions over the U.S.-led invasion of Iraq.

summits.name: Gleneagles
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on scaling up aid, debt relief, health, education, and peace capacity for Africa; creating a long-term dialogue and action plan on climate change, clean energy, and sustainable development; and supporting the Millennium Development Goals and more effective development assistance.
summits.context: The summit continued after the July 7 attacks in London, with leaders issuing a collective statement against terrorism.
outcomes: Scale up aid, debt relief, health, education, and peace capacity for Africa
Create a long-term dialogue and action plan on climate change, clean energy, and sustainable development
Support the Millennium Development Goals and more effective development assistance
Advance trade and global economic growth
Maintain solidarity after the July 7 terrorist bombings in London

summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on responding to weakening growth, financial strains, and high oil and commodity prices; endorsing a long-term goal of at least halving global greenhouse-gas emissions by 2050 for UN negotiation; and coordinating action on the global food-price and food-security crisis.
summits.context: The summit preceded the most acute phase of the global financial crisis later in 2008.

Endorse a long-term goal of at least halving global greenhouse-gas emissions by 2050 for UN negotiation
Coordinate action on the global food-price and food-security crisis
Accelerate progress on Africa, health, and the Millennium Development Goals
Address Zimbabwe, Iran, North Korea, terrorism, and regional security
summits.why_it_matters: The summit preceded the most acute phase of the global financial crisis later in 2008.

summits.name: L'Aquila
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on coordinating recovery from the global financial and economic crisis; strengthening financial regulation and resisting protectionism; and launching the L'Aquila Food Security Initiative with major new financing pledges.
summits.context: The summit was moved from La Maddalena, Sardinia, to L'Aquila after the April 2009 earthquake. The G20 was increasingly central to global macroeconomic crisis management.
outcomes: Coordinate recovery from the global financial and economic crisis
Strengthen financial regulation and resist protectionism
Launch the L'Aquila Food Security Initiative with major new financing pledges
Recognize the scientific case for limiting warming to two degrees Celsius
Advance development, Africa, health, and accountability
Coordinate on nonproliferation and regional crises
summits.why_it_matters: The summit was moved from La Maddalena, Sardinia, to L'Aquila after the April 2009 earthquake. The G20 was increasingly central to global macroeconomic crisis management.

summits.context: Canada hosted the G20 in Toronto immediately after Muskoka, illustrating the division of labor between the G8 and the broader G20 on global economic governance.

Support African growth, food security, and peace and security
Coordinate on nuclear nonproliferation, terrorism, Iran, and North Korea
Address Haiti and other vulnerable states while handing macroeconomic coordination to the adjacent G20 summit

summits.name: Brussels
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on condemning Russia's violation of Ukraine's sovereignty and coordinating sanctions; supporting Ukraine's political transition, economy, and energy security; and strengthening global growth, trade, and financial stability.
summits.context: Russia's planned G8 summit at Sochi on the same dates was canceled after Russia's annexation of Crimea. The other members suspended participation in the G8 and met as the G7 in Brussels; the European Union hosted because the normal 2014 presidency would have been Russia's.
outcomes: Condemn Russia's violation of Ukraine's sovereignty and coordinate sanctions
Support Ukraine's political transition, economy, and energy security
Strengthen global growth, trade, and financial stability
Diversify energy supplies and reduce vulnerability to coercion
Advance climate action toward a 2015 global agreement
Coordinate on development, terrorism, and nonproliferation
summits.why_it_matters: Russia's planned G8 summit at Sochi on the same dates was canceled after Russia's annexation of Crimea. The other members suspended participation in the G8 and met as the G7 in Brussels; the European Union hosted because the normal 2014 presidency would have been Russia's.

summits.name: Elmau
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on maintaining sanctions and diplomatic pressure over Russia's actions in Ukraine; committing to decarbonization of the global economy over the course of the century; and promoting global growth, open trade, and responsible supply chains.
summits.context: This was the first regular annual summit after the return to the G7 format.
outcomes: Maintain sanctions and diplomatic pressure over Russia's actions in Ukraine
Commit to decarbonization of the global economy over the course of the century
Promote global growth, open trade, and responsible supply chains
Strengthen preparedness after Ebola and act on antimicrobial resistance and neglected diseases
Advance the Sustainable Development Goals, food security, women's empowerment, and Africa partnerships
Coordinate against terrorism and violent extremism
summits.why_it_matters: This was the first regular annual summit after the return to the G7 format.

summits.name: Taormina
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on adopting a focused action statement against terrorism and violent extremism; supporting global growth, jobs, innovation, and a rules-based trading system; and addressing migration and refugee flows while emphasizing border security.
summits.context: The communique transparently recorded that the United States was reviewing its climate policy and could not join the other members' climate consensus.

Support global growth, jobs, innovation, and a rules-based trading system
Address migration and refugee flows while emphasizing border security
Deepen partnerships with Africa and improve food security and nutrition
Coordinate on North Korea, Syria, Libya, and Ukraine
Record a split on the Paris climate agreement pending a U.S. policy review
summits.why_it_matters: The communique transparently recorded that the United States was reviewing its climate policy and could not join the other members' climate consensus.

Manage divisions over trade and climate while coordinating on peace and security

summits.name: Biarritz
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on supporting open and fair trade, World Trade Organization reform, and international tax modernization; seeking de-escalation with Iran and preventing an Iranian nuclear weapon; and coordinating on Ukraine, Libya, Hong Kong, and other regional crises.
summits.context: France deliberately avoided a conventional long omnibus communiqué. Leaders issued a one-page declaration, with chair's summaries and thematic documents covering other work.
outcomes: Support open and fair trade, World Trade Organization reform, and international tax modernization
Seek de-escalation with Iran and prevent an Iranian nuclear weapon
Coordinate on Ukraine, Libya, Hong Kong, and other regional crises
Fight inequality and advance gender equality
Deepen partnerships with Africa and the Sahel
Mobilize action on the Amazon fires, biodiversity, oceans, and climate
summits.why_it_matters: France deliberately avoided a conventional long omnibus communiqué. Leaders issued a one-page declaration, with chair's summaries and thematic documents covering other work.

summits.name: United States Virtual
summits.summit_title: U.S.-hosted Virtual G7 Leaders' Meeting on COVID-19
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on coordinating public-health measures against COVID-19; accelerating research, data sharing, treatments, vaccines, and medical-equipment access; and using fiscal and monetary tools to protect jobs and stabilize the world economy.
summits.context: An in-person summit was announced for Camp David on June 10-12, changed to videoconference on March 19, and later postponed indefinitely; it was never held. The March 16 emergency videoconference is the substantive leaders' meeting used for this annual record. Additional G7 calls took place during the U.S. presidency.
outcomes: Coordinate public-health measures against COVID-19
Accelerate research, data sharing, treatments, vaccines, and medical-equipment access
Use fiscal and monetary tools to protect jobs and stabilize the world economy
Keep trade and investment moving and address supply-chain disruption
Coordinate through international institutions and prepare a strong recovery
summits.why_it_matters: Demonstrates both the value and the limits of emergency virtual summitry: leaders rapidly aligned health, research, fiscal, monetary, and trade responses, but the United States never convened the planned in-person annual summit.

documents.title: G7 Leaders' Statement, March 16, 2020
documents.publisher: White House Historical Archive

data_gaps: There was no single in-person annual summit; this record uses the March 16 emergency leaders’ videoconference and documents the cancelled Camp David plan.

summits.name: Carbis Bay
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on ending the COVID-19 pandemic through vaccine sharing and stronger pandemic preparedness; building back better through green and digital recovery, jobs, and a global minimum tax; and launching a values-driven infrastructure partnership for developing countries.
summits.context: The first in-person annual summit since Biarritz in 2019, held with pandemic precautions.
outcomes: End the COVID-19 pandemic through vaccine sharing and stronger pandemic preparedness
Build back better through green and digital recovery, jobs, and a global minimum tax
Launch a values-driven infrastructure partnership for developing countries
Accelerate net-zero, climate finance, biodiversity, and coal transition ahead of COP26
Defend open societies and coordinate on China, Russia, and regional security
Set targets and financing for girls' education
summits.why_it_matters: The first in-person annual summit since Biarritz in 2019, held with pandemic precautions.

Manage energy insecurity, price shocks, inflation, and supply-chain disruption
Address global food insecurity through the Global Alliance for Food Security
Launch the Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment
Create a Climate Club and accelerate a clean and just transition
Strengthen democratic resilience and international partnerships

summits.name: Apulia
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on backing Ukraine and agreeing a roughly $50 billion ERA loan using future revenues from immobilized Russian sovereign assets; coordinating on Gaza, the Middle East, and broader regional security; and building equal partnerships with Africa through infrastructure, energy, and the Mattei Plan-PGII connection.
summits.context: Pope Francis addressed the outreach session on artificial intelligence, the first participation by a pope in a G7 leaders' summit.
outcomes: Back Ukraine and agree a roughly $50 billion ERA loan using future revenues from immobilized Russian sovereign assets
Coordinate on Gaza, the Middle East, and broader regional security
Build equal partnerships with Africa through infrastructure, energy, and the Mattei Plan-PGII connection
Address migration and migrant smuggling
Advance safe, secure, and trustworthy artificial intelligence and its effects on work
Strengthen economic security, food security, climate, and energy transition
summits.why_it_matters: Pope Francis addressed the outreach session on artificial intelligence, the first participation by a pope in a G7 leaders' summit.

summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on launching a Critical Minerals Action Plan and strengthening energy and supply-chain security; promoting responsible AI adoption, quantum cooperation, and the digital transition; and supporting Ukraine and coordinating on Israel-Iran escalation and other geopolitical crises.
summits.context: Canada did not issue a conventional omnibus consensus communique. The public record consists of a chair's summary plus issue-specific statements and plans. The summit marked fifty years since Rambouillet.

Promote responsible AI adoption, quantum cooperation, and the digital transition
Support Ukraine and coordinate on Israel-Iran escalation and other geopolitical crises
Create a multilateral framework for wildfire prevention, response, and recovery
Counter transnational repression, migrant smuggling, and foreign interference
Mobilize investment and partnerships for shared prosperity

summits.name: Évian
summits.summary: The leaders’ agenda centered on coordinating on Ukraine, Russia, the Middle East, and Indo-Pacific security; pursuing more balanced, durable, and resilient growth by addressing global imbalances; and securing and diversifying critical-mineral supply chains through investment, transparency, stockpiling, and recycling.
summits.context: Verified after completion against the Élysée summit dossier and results pages on July 10, 2026. France reported nine leaders’ texts rather than one omnibus communiqué. The next rotating presidency is the United States in 2027.
outcomes: Coordinate on Ukraine, Russia, the Middle East, and Indo-Pacific security
Pursue more balanced, durable, and resilient growth by addressing global imbalances
Secure and diversify critical-mineral supply chains through investment, transparency, stockpiling, and recycling
Renew international partnerships and development finance around mutual benefit
Promote safe and effective AI deployment and a safer digital space for minors
Act jointly on migrant and drug trafficking, cancer research, and an Ebola outbreak
summits.why_it_matters: The final summit before the 2027 U.S. presidency created a nine-text implementation record spanning security, global imbalances, critical minerals, AI and digital safety, partnerships, health, and transnational crime.
